1→;; blink.l24 -- Blink LED D2 on COR24-TB
     2→;;
     3→;; Shows: Memory-mapped I/O via set-leds, delay spin loop, tail-recursive blink
     4→;; Prelude: standard (uses set-leds, delay)
     5→;; Stack: 3 KB (default)
     6→;; Run: just demo-blink
     7→;; Note: Requires --speed 500000 for timing. Ctrl-] to exit.
     8→
     9→(define blink (lambda (on-ms off-ms) (begin (set-leds 1) (println 'on) (delay on-ms) (set-leds 0) (println 'off) (delay off-ms) (blink on-ms off-ms))))
    10→(blink 1000 1000)
    11→

1→;; tco.l24 -- Tail-call optimization
     2→;;
     3→;; Shows: Constant-stack recursive functions: countdown, sum-accumulator, tail-recursive fibonacci
     4→;; Prelude: minimal (no prelude functions needed)
     5→;; Stack: 3 KB (default)
     6→;; Run: just eval demos/tco.l24
     7→
     8→(define countdown (lambda (n) (if (= n 0) 'done (countdown (- n 1)))))
     9→(countdown 100)
    10→
    11→;; Sum 1..n via tail-recursive accumulator
    12→(define sum-acc (lambda (n acc) (if (= n 0) acc (sum-acc (- n 1) (+ acc n)))))
    13→(sum-acc 100 0)
    14→
    15→;; Fibonacci via tail recursion
    16→(define fib-acc (lambda (n a b) (if (= n 0) a (fib-acc (- n 1) b (+ a b)))))
    17→(fib-acc 10 0 1)
    18→
    19→(exit)
    20→

1→# Demos
     2→
     3→All demos are in the `demos/` directory. Run with `just eval demos/<name>.l24` or the named recipe where available.
     4→
     5→## Language Features
     6→
     7→| Demo | Features | Command | Description |
     8→|------|----------|---------|-------------|
     9→| `quasiquote.l24` | `` ` ``, `,`, `,@` | `just eval demos/quasiquote.l24` | Quasiquote templates, unquote, splicing, macro code generation |
    10→| `tco.l24` | Tail-call optimization | `just eval demos/tco.l24` | Countdown, sum-accumulator, tail-recursive fibonacci — constant stack |
    11→| `variadic.l24` | Rest parameters | `just eval demos/variadic.l24` | `(lambda args ...)`, `(lambda (a . rest) ...)`, variadic sum |
    12→| `strings.l24` | String type | `just eval demos/strings.l24` | Literals, length, append, equality, display, strings in lists |
    13→| `macros.l24` | let, cond, and, or | `just eval demos/macros.l24` | let bindings, multi-way cond, short-circuit and/or, classify function |
    14→| `anaphora.l24` | Unhygienic macros | `just eval demos/anaphora.l24` | aif, awhen, aand — `it` captures test result for use in body |
    15→| `mutation.l24` | set!, closures over mutable state | `just eval demos/mutation.l24` | Counters, memoized fibonacci, accumulator factory |
    16→| `threading.l24` | `->`, `->>` | `just eval demos/threading.l24` | Thread-first/last pipelines, data transformation |
    17→| `lazy.l24` | Lazy sequences | `just eval demos/lazy.l24` | Infinite naturals, powers of 2, lazy map/filter, lazy fibonacci |

1→# tml24c
     2→
     3→Tiny Macro Lisp for COR24 — a minimal Lisp-1 with lexical scope, unhygienic defmacro, closures, mark-sweep GC, and tail-call optimization. Compiles to COR24 24-bit RISC assembly.
     4→
     5→Built on:
     6→- [tc24r](https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r) — Tiny COR24 C compiler (Rust)
     7→- [cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs) — COR24 assembler and emulator
     8→
     9→## Quick Start
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→just build       # compile
    13→just test        # run test suite
    14→just run         # interactive REPL (Ctrl-] to exit)
    15→just eval <file> # evaluate a .l24 file
    16→```
    17→
    18→## Language
    19→
    20→- **Types**: fixnums, symbols, cons pairs, strings, closures, macros, primitives
    21→- **Special forms**: `quote`, `if`, `define`, `set!`, `lambda`, `defmacro`, `begin`, `quasiquote`
    22→- **Standard prelude**: 60+ functions and macros including `map`, `filter`, `reduce`, `let`, `cond`, `and`, `or`, `->`, `->>`, lazy sequences, anaphoric macros, and more
    23→- **Tail-call optimization**: `if`, `begin`, and closure application
    24→- **Conservative GC**: scans C stack for heap pointers — no manual root management
    25→- **Multi-line REPL**: paren-depth tracking across newlines
    26→- **I/O**: `peek`/`poke` for memory-mapped hardware, `display` for UART output
    27→
    28→## Demos
    29→
    30→See [docs/demos.md](docs/demos.md) for the full list. Highlights:
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→just demo-bottles   # 99 Bottles of Beer (macro + tail recursion)
    34→just demo-bottles2  # 99 Bottles (trampoline mutual recursion)
    35→just eval demos/lazy.l24       # infinite sequences
    36→just eval demos/anaphora.l24   # anaphoric macros (aif, awhen)
    37→just eval demos/threading.l24  # -> and ->> pipelines
    38→just eval demos/mutation.l24   # set!, counters, memoization
    39→```
    40→

    42→

    44→|----------|-------------|
    45→| [demos.md](docs/demos.md) | Demo descriptions and commands |

1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)
     6→
     7→This project uses AgentRail. Every session follows this exact sequence:
     8→
     9→### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else)
    10→```bash
    11→agentrail next
    12→```
    13→Read the output carefully. It tells you your current step, prompt, skill docs, and past trajectories.
    14→
    15→### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output)
    16→```bash
    17→agentrail begin
    18→```
    19→
    20→### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
    21→Do NOT ask the user "want me to proceed?" or "shall I start?". The step prompt IS your instruction. Execute it.
    22→
    23→### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done)
    24→Commit your code changes with git.
    25→
    26→### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing, after committing)
    27→```bash
    28→agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \\
    29→  --reward 1 \\
    30→  --actions "tools and approach used"
    31→```
    32→If the step failed: `--reward -1 --failure-mode "what went wrong"`
    33→If the saga is finished: add `--done`
    34→
    35→### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue working)
    36→Do NOT make any further code changes after running agentrail complete.
    37→Any changes after complete are untracked and invisible to the next session.
    38→If you see more work to do, it belongs in the NEXT step, not this session.
    39→
    40→Do NOT skip any of these steps. The next session depends on your trajectory recording.
    41→
    42→## Project
    43→
    44→Tiny Macro Lisp (tml24c): a minimal Lisp-1 with lexical scope, unhygienic defmacro, closures, and mark-sweep GC. Compiles to COR24 24-bit RISC assembly.
    45→
    46→## Related Projects
    47→
    48→- `~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs` -- COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust)
    49→- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r` -- Tiny COR24 compiler (Rust)
    50→- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding` -- Coding skills domain
    51→
    52→## Available Task Types
    53→
    54→`c-project-init`, `c-compile-fix`, `lisp-define-form`, `pre-commit`
    55→
    56→## Build
    57→
    58→Uses [just](https://github.com/casey/just) as the command runner.
    59→
    60→```bash
    61→just build       # build test binary
    62→just build-repl  # build REPL binary
    63→just test        # run test suite
    64→just run         # interactive REPL (Ctrl-] to exit)
    65→just eval <file> # evaluate a .l24 file
    66→just demo-blink  # LED blink demo
    67→just clean       # clean build artifacts
    68→```
    69→
    70→Compiler flags: `-Wall -Wextra -Werror -std=c11`. Never suppress warnings.

1→;; quasiquote.l24 -- Quasiquote, unquote, and splicing
     2→;;
     3→;; Shows: ` (quasiquote), , (unquote), ,@ (unquote-splicing), macro code generation with templates
     4→;; Prelude: standard
     5→;; Stack: 3 KB (default)
     6→;; Run: just eval demos/quasiquote.l24
     7→
     8→`(hello world)
     9→
    10→;; Unquote: evaluate inside a template
    11→(define name 'lisp)
    12→`(hello ,name)
    13→
    14→;; Splicing: insert list elements into template
    15→(define nums (list 1 2 3))
    16→`(before ,@nums after)
    17→
    18→;; Macro with quasiquote (much cleaner than list/cons)
    19→(defmacro my-when (cond body) `(if ,cond ,body nil))
    20→(my-when (< 1 2) 'yes)
    21→(my-when (< 2 1) 'yes)
    22→
    23→;; Swap macro
    24→(defmacro swap (a b) `(list ,b ,a))
    25→(swap 1 2)
    26→
    27→;; Code generation with quasiquote
    28→(define make-adder-code (lambda (n) `(lambda (x) (+ x ,n))))
    29→(make-adder-code 5)
    30→
    31→(exit)
    32→

1→;; bottles.l24 -- 99 Bottles of Beer (macro + tail recursion)
     2→;;
     3→;; Shows: defmacro for pluralization, display-based output, tail-recursive sing, number->string
     4→;; Prelude: standard
     5→;; Stack: 3 KB (default)
     6→;; Run: just demo-bottles
     7→
     8→(defmacro bottles (n) `(begin (display (number->string ,n)) (if (= 1 ,n) (display " bottle") (display " bottles"))))
     9→
    10→;; Display one verse
    11→(define line1 (lambda (n) (begin (bottles n) (display " of beer on the wall, ") (bottles n) (display " of beer.\n"))))
    12→
    13→(define line2 (lambda (n) (begin (display "Take one down, pass it around, ") (bottles (- n 1)) (display " of beer on the wall.\n\n"))))
    14→
    15→;; Sing from n down to 0
    16→(define sing (lambda (n) (begin (line1 n) (if (= n 0) (display "Go buy some more!\n") (begin (line2 n) (sing (- n 1)))))))
    17→
    18→(sing 5)
    19→
    20→(exit)
    21→

1→;; lazy.l24 -- Lazy sequences
     2→;;
     3→;; Shows: Infinite naturals, powers of 2, lazy-map, lazy-filter, iterate, take-while, drop-while, lazy fibonacci
     4→;; Prelude: full (uses lazy-cons, lazy-take, lazy-map, lazy-filter, iterate, lazy-range)
     5→;; Stack: 8 KB recommended (--stack-kilobytes 8)
     6→;; Run: just eval-full demos/lazy.l24
     7→
     8→(lazy-take 8 (lazy-range 0))
     9→
    10→;; Powers of 2 via iterate
    11→(lazy-take 5 (iterate (lambda (x) (* x 2)) 1))
    12→
    13→;; Lazy map over infinite sequence
    14→(lazy-take 5 (lazy-map (lambda (x) (+ x 100)) (lazy-range 0)))
    15→
    16→;; Lazy filter: first 3 positives from -3..
    17→(lazy-take 3 (lazy-filter positive? (lazy-range -3)))
    18→
    19→;; take-while / drop-while on eager lists
    20→(take-while (lambda (x) (< x 5)) (list 1 2 3 4 5 6 7))
    21→(drop-while (lambda (x) (< x 5)) (list 1 2 3 4 5 6 7))
    22→
    23→;; Fibonacci via iterate on pairs (lazy-map is expensive, use 4)
    24→(define fib-step (lambda (p) (list (cadr p) (+ (car p) (cadr p)))))
    25→(lazy-take 4 (lazy-map car (iterate fib-step (list 0 1))))
    26→
    27→(exit)
    28→

1→;; scheme.l24 -- Scheme-flavored Lisp demo
     2→;;
     3→;; Shows: #t/#f booleans, cond with else, let/let*, define-fn shorthand, equal?, boolean?, list?, even?/odd?, factorial, tail-recursive sum
     4→;; Prelude: scheme (compiled)
     5→;; Stack: 3 KB (default)
     6→;; Run: just eval-scheme demos/scheme.l24
     7→
     8→(display "Booleans: ")
     9→(display #t)
    10→(display " ")
    11→(display #f)
    12→(newline)
    13→
    14→;; cond with else
    15→(define-fn (classify n)
    16→  (cond
    17→    ((negative? n) "negative")
    18→    ((zero? n) "zero")
    19→    (else "positive")))
    20→(display (classify -5))
    21→(display " ")
    22→(display (classify 0))
    23→(display " ")
    24→(display (classify 7))
    25→(newline)
    26→
    27→;; let and let*
    28→(display "let: ")
    29→(display (let ((x 10) (y 20)) (+ x y)))
    30→(newline)
    31→
    32→(display "let*: ")
    33→(display (let* ((x 10) (y (* x 2))) (+ x y)))
    34→(newline)
    35→
    36→;; Higher-order functions
    37→(display "map: ")
    38→(display (map (lambda (x) (* x x)) '(1 2 3 4 5)))
    39→(newline)
    40→
    41→(display "filter: ")
    42→(display (filter even? '(1 2 3 4 5 6)))
    43→(newline)
    44→
    45→;; equal? (deep equality)
    46→(display "equal?: ")
    47→(display (equal? '(1 (2 3)) '(1 (2 3))))
    48→(display " ")
    49→(display (equal? '(1 2) '(1 3)))
    50→(newline)
    51→
    52→;; Predicates
    53→(display "boolean?: ")
    54→(display (boolean? #t))
    55→(display " ")
    56→(display (boolean? 42))
    57→(newline)
    58→
    59→(display "list?: ")
    60→(display (list? '(1 2 3)))
    61→(display " ")
    62→(display (list? (cons 1 2)))
    63→(newline)
    64→
    65→;; define-fn shorthand
    66→(define-fn (factorial n)
    67→  (if (= n 0) 1 (* n (factorial (- n 1)))))
    68→(display "fact 6: ")
    69→(display (factorial 6))
    70→(newline)
    71→
    72→;; Tail-recursive accumulator
    73→(define sum-helper (lambda (i acc) (if (= i 0) acc (sum-helper (- i 1) (+ acc i)))))
    74→(define-fn (sum-to n) (sum-helper n 0))
    75→(display "sum 1..100: ")
    76→(display (sum-to 100))
    77→(newline)
    78→
    79→(exit)
    80→

1→;; standard.l24 -- Standard prelude
     2→;; Core Lisp functions. Works with --stack-kilobytes 3 (default).
     3→
     4→;; List operations
     5→(define map (lambda (f lst) (if (null? lst) lst (cons (f (car lst)) (map f (cdr lst))))))
     6→(define filter (lambda (p lst) (if (null? lst) lst (if (p (car lst)) (cons (car lst) (filter p (cdr lst))) (filter p (cdr lst))))))
     7→(define reduce (lambda (f init lst) (if (null? lst) init (reduce f (f init (car lst)) (cdr lst)))))
     8→(define foldr (lambda (f init lst) (if (null? lst) init (f (car lst) (foldr f init (cdr lst))))))
     9→(define length (lambda (lst) (if (null? lst) 0 (+ 1 (length (cdr lst))))))
    10→(define append (lambda (a b) (if (null? a) b (cons (car a) (append (cdr a) b)))))
    11→(define reverse (lambda (lst) (reduce (lambda (acc x) (cons x acc)) nil lst)))
    12→(define nth (lambda (n lst) (if (= n 0) (car lst) (nth (- n 1) (cdr lst)))))
    13→
    14→;; List accessors
    15→(define cadr (lambda (x) (car (cdr x))))
    16→(define caddr (lambda (x) (car (cdr (cdr x)))))
    17→(define caar (lambda (x) (car (car x))))
    18→(define cdar (lambda (x) (cdr (car x))))
    19→
    20→;; Macros
    21→(defmacro when (cond body) `(if ,cond ,body nil))
    22→(defmacro unless (cond body) `(if ,cond nil ,body))
    23→(defmacro let (bindings body) `((lambda ,(map car bindings) ,body) ,@(map cadr bindings)))
    24→(defmacro and (a b) `(if ,a ,b nil))
    25→(defmacro or (a b) `(if ,a ,a ,b))
    26→(define cond-expand (lambda (clauses) (if (null? clauses) nil (if (eq? (caar clauses) 't) (cadr (car clauses)) `(if ,(caar clauses) ,(cadr (car clauses)) ,(cond-expand (cdr clauses)))))))
    27→(defmacro cond clauses (cond-expand clauses))
    28→
    29→;; Comparison
    30→(define > (lambda (a b) (< b a)))
    31→(define >= (lambda (a b) (not (< a b))))
    32→(define <= (lambda (a b) (not (< b a))))
    33→
    34→;; Numeric
    35→(define zero? (lambda (n) (= n 0)))
    36→(define positive? (lambda (n) (< 0 n)))
    37→(define negative? (lambda (n) (< n 0)))
    38→(define abs (lambda (n) (if (< n 0) (- 0 n) n)))
    39→(define min (lambda (a b) (if (< a b) a b)))
    40→(define max (lambda (a b) (if (< a b) b a)))
    41→
    42→;; Function combinators
    43→(define identity (lambda (x) x))
    44→(define complement (lambda (f) (lambda (x) (not (f x)))))
    45→(define compose (lambda (f g) (lambda (x) (f (g x)))))
    46→
    47→;; String utilities
    48→(define ->str (lambda (x) (cond ((string? x) x) ((number? x) (number->string x)) (t ""))))
    49→(define str2 (lambda (a b) (string-append (->str a) (->str b))))
    50→(define str (lambda args (reduce str2 "" args)))

1→;; full.l24 -- Full prelude (includes standard + extras)
     2→;; Requires --stack-kilobytes 8 for deep call chains.
     3→
     4→;; Threading macros
     5→(defmacro -> (x form) (cons (car form) (cons x (cdr form))))
     6→(defmacro ->> (x form) (append form (list x)))
     7→
     8→;; List construction
     9→(define range-helper (lambda (i n) (if (= i n) nil (cons i (range-helper (+ i 1) n)))))
    10→(define range (lambda (n) (range-helper 0 n)))
    11→(define repeat (lambda (n x) (if (= n 0) nil (cons x (repeat (- n 1) x)))))
    12→(define take (lambda (n lst) (if (= n 0) nil (if (null? lst) nil (cons (car lst) (take (- n 1) (cdr lst)))))))
    13→(define drop (lambda (n lst) (if (= n 0) lst (if (null? lst) nil (drop (- n 1) (cdr lst))))))
    14→(define zip (lambda (a b) (if (null? a) nil (if (null? b) nil (cons (list (car a) (car b)) (zip (cdr a) (cdr b)))))))
    15→(define flatten (lambda (lst) (if (null? lst) nil (if (pair? (car lst)) (append (flatten (car lst)) (flatten (cdr lst))) (cons (car lst) (flatten (cdr lst)))))))
    16→
    17→;; Predicate combinators
    18→(define constantly (lambda (x) (lambda (y) x)))
    19→(define every? (lambda (p lst) (if (null? lst) t (if (p (car lst)) (every? p (cdr lst)) nil))))
    20→(define some (lambda (p lst) (if (null? lst) nil (if (p (car lst)) (car lst) (some p (cdr lst))))))
    21→(define none? (lambda (p lst) (every? (complement p) lst)))
    22→
    23→;; Utility functions
    24→(define partial (lambda (f . args) (lambda rest (apply f (append args rest)))))
    25→(define juxt (lambda (f g) (lambda (x) (list (f x) (g x)))))
    26→(defmacro doseq (binding body) `(for-each (lambda (,(car binding)) ,body) ,(cadr binding)))
    27→(defmacro dotimes (binding body) `(for-each (lambda (,(car binding)) ,body) (range ,(cadr binding))))
    28→
    29→;; Trampoline
    30→(define trampoline (lambda (f) (let ((r (f))) (if (fn? r) (trampoline r) r))))
    31→
    32→;; Lazy sequences
    33→(define lazy-cons (lambda (h thk) (cons h (cons 'thunk thk))))
    34→(define lazy? (lambda (s) (and (pair? s) (and (pair? (cdr s)) (eq? (car (cdr s)) 'thunk)))))
    35→(define lazy-car car)
    36→(define lazy-cdr (lambda (s) (if (lazy? s) (let ((v ((cdr (cdr s))))) (begin (set! s (cons (car s) v)) v)) (cdr s))))
    37→(define lazy-take (lambda (n s) (if (= n 0) nil (if (null? s) nil (cons (lazy-car s) (lazy-take (- n 1) (lazy-cdr s)))))))
    38→(define lazy-map (lambda (f s) (if (null? s) nil (lazy-cons (f (lazy-car s)) (lambda () (lazy-map f (lazy-cdr s)))))))
    39→(define lazy-filter (lambda (p s) (if (null? s) nil (if (p (lazy-car s)) (lazy-cons (lazy-car s) (lambda () (lazy-filter p (lazy-cdr s)))) (lazy-filter p (lazy-cdr s))))))
    40→(define iterate (lambda (f x) (lazy-cons x (lambda () (iterate f (f x))))))
    41→(define lazy-range (lambda (n) (iterate (lambda (x) (+ x 1)) n)))
    42→(define take-while (lambda (p lst) (if (null? lst) nil (if (p (car lst)) (cons (car lst) (take-while p (cdr lst))) nil))))
    43→(define drop-while (lambda (p lst) (if (null? lst) nil (if (p (car lst)) (drop-while p (cdr lst)) lst))))
    44→
    45→;; Anaphoric macros
    46→(defmacro aif (test then else) `(let ((it ,test)) (if it ,then ,else)))
    47→(defmacro awhen (test body) `(let ((it ,test)) (if it ,body nil)))
    48→(defmacro aand (a b) `(let ((it ,a)) (if it ,b nil)))
    49→
    50→;; Boolean aliases

1→;; scheme.l24 -- Scheme-flavored prelude
     2→;;
     3→;; R7RS-inspired naming conventions and idioms on top of tml24c.
     4→;; Load with: tml24c custom prelude/scheme.l24
     5→;;   or: just run-custom prelude/scheme.l24
     6→;;
     7→;; Supports both #t/#f and t/nil. No char type or call/cc.
     8→
     9→;; === Core list operations (same as standard, Scheme names) ===
    10→
    11→(define map (lambda (f lst) (if (null? lst) lst (cons (f (car lst)) (map f (cdr lst))))))
    12→(define filter (lambda (p lst) (if (null? lst) lst (if (p (car lst)) (cons (car lst) (filter p (cdr lst))) (filter p (cdr lst))))))
    13→(define for-each (lambda (f lst) (if (null? lst) (begin) (begin (f (car lst)) (for-each f (cdr lst))))))
    14→(define length (lambda (lst) (if (null? lst) 0 (+ 1 (length (cdr lst))))))
    15→(define append (lambda (a b) (if (null? a) b (cons (car a) (append (cdr a) b)))))
    16→(define reverse (lambda (lst) (define go (lambda (in out) (if (null? in) out (go (cdr in) (cons (car in) out))))) (go lst '())))
    17→(define list-ref (lambda (lst n) (if (= n 0) (car lst) (list-ref (cdr lst) (- n 1)))))
    18→
    19→;; === Scheme predicates ===
    20→
    21→(define boolean? (lambda (x) (if (eq? x #t) #t (null? x))))
    22→(define list? (lambda (x) (if (null? x) #t (if (pair? x) (list? (cdr x)) #f))))
    23→(define equal? (lambda (a b) (if (eq? a b) #t (if (pair? a) (if (pair? b) (if (equal? (car a) (car b)) (equal? (cdr a) (cdr b)) #f) #f) (if (string? a) (if (string? b) (string=? a b) #f) #f)))))
    24→(define zero? (lambda (n) (= n 0)))
    25→(define even? (lambda (n) (= (% n 2) 0)))
    26→(define odd? (lambda (n) (not (= (% n 2) 0))))
    27→
    28→;; === Scheme numeric ===
    29→
    30→(define abs (lambda (n) (if (< n 0) (- 0 n) n)))
    31→(define max (lambda (a b) (if (< a b) b a)))
    32→(define min (lambda (a b) (if (< a b) a b)))
    33→(define modulo %)
    34→(define remainder %)
    35→(define positive? (lambda (n) (< 0 n)))
    36→(define negative? (lambda (n) (< n 0)))
    37→
    38→;; === Scheme-style let/let*/letrec ===
    39→
    40→(define cadr (lambda (x) (car (cdr x))))
    41→(define caar (lambda (x) (car (car x))))
    42→(define cdar (lambda (x) (cdr (car x))))
    43→(define caddr (lambda (x) (car (cdr (cdr x)))))
    44→(define reduce (lambda (f init lst) (if (null? lst) init (reduce f (f init (car lst)) (cdr lst)))))
    45→
    46→(defmacro let (bindings body) `((lambda ,(map car bindings) ,body) ,@(map cadr bindings)))
    47→
    48→;; let*: sequential binding (each sees previous)
    49→(define let*-expand (lambda (bindings body) (if (null? (cdr bindings)) `(let (,(car bindings)) ,body) `(let (,(car bindings)) ,(let*-expand (cdr bindings) body)))))
    50→(defmacro let* (bindings body) (let*-expand bindings body))

1→;; continuations.l24 — Escape continuations and error handling demo
     2→;; Run with: just eval demos/continuations.l24
     3→
     4→;; === Part 1: catch/throw — non-local exit ===
     5→
     6→(display "--- catch/throw ---")
     7→(newline)
     8→
     9→;; Basic: throw jumps out of nested computation
    10→(display "catch basic: ")
    11→(println (catch 'done (begin (+ 1 2) (throw 'done 42) (+ 3 4))))
    12→
    13→;; Nested: throw to outer bypasses inner catch
    14→(display "nested throw: ")
    15→(println (catch 'outer
    16→  (catch 'inner
    17→    (begin
    18→      (throw 'outer 'escaped)
    19→      'never-reached))))
    20→
    21→;; === Part 2: call/ec — escape continuations ===
    22→
    23→(display "--- call/ec ---")
    24→(newline)
    25→
    26→;; find-first: early return from for-each
    27→(define (find-first pred lst)
    28→  (call/ec (lambda (return)
    29→    (for-each (lambda (x)
    30→      (if (pred x) (return x) nil))
    31→      lst)
    32→    nil)))
    33→
    34→(display "find 3 in (1..5): ")
    35→(println (find-first (lambda (x) (= x 3)) '(1 2 3 4 5)))
    36→
    37→(display "find 9 in (1..3): ")
    38→(println (find-first (lambda (x) (= x 9)) '(1 2 3)))
    39→
    40→;; any?: short-circuit predicate test
    41→(define (any? pred lst)
    42→  (call/ec (lambda (return)
    43→    (for-each (lambda (x)
    44→      (if (pred x) (return t) nil))
    45→      lst)
    46→    nil)))
    47→
    48→(display "any even in (1 3 4 7): ")
    49→(println (any? (lambda (x) (= (% x 2) 0)) '(1 3 4 7)))
    50→
    51→(display "any even in (1 3 5 7): ")
    52→(println (any? (lambda (x) (= (% x 2) 0)) '(1 3 5 7)))
    53→
    54→;; === Part 3: Error handling ===
    55→
    56→(display "--- error handling ---")
    57→(newline)
    58→
    59→;; safe-div: raises on division by zero
    60→(define (safe-div a b)
    61→  (if (= b 0)
    62→    (raise 'div-by-zero)
    63→    (/ a b)))
    64→
    65→(display "10/2 = ")
    66→(println (with-handler
    67→  (lambda (e) (begin (display "caught: ") (println e) -1))
    68→  (lambda () (safe-div 10 2))))
    69→
    70→(display "10/0 = ")
    71→(println (with-handler
    72→  (lambda (e) (begin (display "caught: ") (println e) -1))
    73→  (lambda () (safe-div 10 0))))
    74→
    75→;; Nested handlers: inner catches first
    76→(display "nested: ")
    77→(println (with-handler
    78→  (lambda (e) 'outer-caught)
    79→  (lambda ()
    80→    (with-handler
    81→      (lambda (e) 'inner-caught)
    82→      (lambda () (raise 'oops))))))
    83→
    84→;; === Part 4: Practical — safe list operations ===
    85→
    86→(define (safe-nth n lst)
    87→  (if (null? lst)
    88→    (raise 'index-out-of-bounds)
    89→    (if (= n 0)
    90→      (car lst)
    91→      (safe-nth (- n 1) (cdr lst)))))
    92→
    93→(display "nth 2 of (a b c): ")
    94→(println (with-handler
    95→  (lambda (e) (begin (display "err: ") e))
    96→  (lambda () (safe-nth 2 '(a b c)))))
    97→
    98→(display "nth 5 of (a b c): ")
    99→(println (with-handler
   100→  (lambda (e) (begin (display "err: ") e))
   101→  (lambda () (safe-nth 5 '(a b c)))))
   102→
   103→;; === Part 5: guard — pattern-matched error handling ===
   104→
   105→(display "--- guard ---")
   106→(newline)
   107→
   108→;; guard with clause matching
   109→(display "div 10/0 guarded: ")
   110→(println (guard (e
   111→    ((eq? e 'div-by-zero) 'caught-zero))
   112→  (safe-div 10 0)))
   113→
   114→;; guard with else
   115→(display "guard else: ")
   116→(println (guard (e
   117→    ((eq? e 'expected) 'nope)
   118→    (else 'fallback))
   119→  (raise 'surprise)))
   120→
   121→;; guard passes through on success
   122→(display "guard ok: ")
   123→(println (guard (e
   124→    ((eq? e 'div-by-zero) -1))
   125→  (safe-div 20 4)))
   126→
   127→;; === Part 6: dynamic-wind — guaranteed cleanup ===
   128→
   129→(display "--- dynamic-wind ---")
   130→(newline)
   131→
   132→;; Normal exit: before, body, after all run
   133→(define dw-trace nil)
   134→(dynamic-wind
   135→  (lambda () (set! dw-trace (cons 'enter dw-trace)))
   136→  (lambda () (set! dw-trace (cons 'body dw-trace)))
   137→  (lambda () (set! dw-trace (cons 'exit dw-trace))))
   138→(display "normal: ")
   139→(println dw-trace)
   140→
   141→;; Non-local exit: after still runs
   142→(set! dw-trace nil)
   143→(catch 'abort
   144→  (dynamic-wind
   145→    (lambda () (set! dw-trace (cons 'enter dw-trace)))
   146→    (lambda () (throw 'abort 'bailed))
   147→    (lambda () (set! dw-trace (cons 'cleanup dw-trace)))))
   148→(display "thrown: ")
   149→(println dw-trace)
   150→
   151→;; === Part 7: unwind-protect — guaranteed cleanup (CL-style) ===
   152→
   153→(display "--- unwind-protect ---")
   154→(newline)
   155→
   156→;; Normal exit: cleanup runs after body
   157→(define up-result nil)
   158→(define up-val
   159→  (unwind-protect
   160→    (begin (set! up-result 'ok) 42)
   161→    (set! up-result (list up-result 'cleaned))))
   162→(display "normal: val=")
   163→(print up-val)
   164→(display " result=")
   165→(println up-result)
   166→
   167→;; Throw exit: cleanup still runs
   168→(set! up-result nil)
   169→(catch 'abort
   170→  (unwind-protect
   171→    (begin (set! up-result 'started) (throw 'abort 'bailed))
   172→    (set! up-result (list up-result 'cleaned))))
   173→(display "thrown: ")
   174→(println up-result)
   175→
   176→(display "--- done ---")
   177→(newline)
   178→

1→# Prelude Choices
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→The tml24c prelude is compiled into the REPL binary as C strings evaluated at startup. Different use cases need different prelude sizes. This document proposes 4 prelude tiers.
     6→
     7→## Prelude Tiers
     8→
     9→### 1. Tiny (minimal)
    10→
    11→**Use case**: Maximum heap/stack headroom, debugging, understanding core behavior.
    12→**Stack**: Works with `--stack small` (3KB).
    13→**Instruction cost**: ~500K to load.
    14→
    15→Includes only what's needed for basic Lisp programming:
    16→
    17→```
    18→Primitives only (from C): +, -, *, /, %, <, =, cons, car, cdr, list,
    19→  null?, pair?, atom?, eq?, not, print, println, newline, display,
    20→  number?, string?, fn?, exit, peek, poke, delay, gc, heap-used,
    21→  heap-size, number->string, apply, string-length, string-ref,
    22→  string-append, string=?
    23→```
    24→
    25→No prelude definitions — just the C-registered primitives.
    26→
    27→### 2. Standard (default)
    28→
    29→**Use case**: General-purpose Lisp programming on COR24-TB.
    30→**Stack**: Works with `--stack small` (3KB).
    31→**Instruction cost**: ~3M to load.
    32→
    33→Adds core Lisp functions:
    34→
    35→```
    36→From tiny, plus:
    37→  map, filter, reduce, foldr, length, append, reverse, nth
    38→  cadr, caddr, caar, cdar
    39→  >, >=, <=, zero?, positive?, negative?, abs, min, max
    40→  when, unless, let, cond, and, or
    41→  identity, complement, compose
    42→  for-each
    43→  ->str, str2, str
    44→  set!
    45→  IO constants: IO-LED, IO-SWITCH, etc.
    46→  set-leds, get-leds, s2-pressed?
    47→```
    48→
    49→### 3. Full (extended)
    50→
    51→**Use case**: Clojure-style programming, demos, complex applications.
    52→**Stack**: Requires `--stack large` (8KB).
    53→**Instruction cost**: ~8M to load.
    54→
    55→Adds everything in standard, plus:
    56→
    57→```
    58→From standard, plus:
    59→  range, repeat, take, drop, zip, flatten
    60→  range-down, interleave3
    61→  every?, some, none?
    62→  constantly, partial, juxt
    63→  -> (thread-first), ->> (thread-last)
    64→  doseq, dotimes
    65→  lazy-cons, lazy-car, lazy-cdr, lazy?, lazy-take
    66→  lazy-map, lazy-filter, iterate, lazy-range
    67→  take-while, drop-while
    68→  assoc, get
    69→  trampoline, fn?
    70→  aif, awhen, aand, acond (anaphoric macros)
    71→  cond-expand (cond helper)
    72→  true, false (aliases)
    73→  for-each
    74→```
    75→
    76→### 4. Experimental
    77→
    78→**Use case**: Testing new features, bleeding-edge development.
    79→**Stack**: Requires `--stack large` (8KB).
    80→**Instruction cost**: ~10M to load.
    81→
    82→Everything in full, plus features that are expensive or not fully tested:
    83→
    84→```
    85→From full, plus:
    86→  Multi-methods (defmulti, defmethod, invoke-multi)
    87→  Memoize (make-memo)
    88→  Atom/swap!/deref
    89→  String formatting utilities
    90→  Debug/trace primitives (when added)
    91→```
    92→
    93→## Implementation Approach
    94→
    95→### Option A: Separate source files (simplest)
    96→
    97→```
    98→src/repl-tiny.c     → build/repl-tiny.s
    99→src/repl.c          → build/repl.s        (standard, current)
   100→src/repl-full.c     → build/repl-full.s
   101→src/repl-exp.c      → build/repl-exp.s
   102→```
   103→
   104→Justfile recipes:
   105→```
   106→run:           # standard prelude
   107→run-tiny:      # minimal
   108→run-full:      # full prelude, --stack large
   109→run-exp:       # experimental, --stack large
   110→```
   111→
   112→### Option B: Prelude as .l24 files (flexible)
   113→
   114→```
   115→prelude/tiny.l24
   116→prelude/standard.l24
   117→prelude/full.l24
   118→prelude/experimental.l24
   119→```
   120→
   121→A bare REPL (`src/repl-bare.c`) with no prelude loads the selected `.l24` file via UART before interactive input:
   122→
   123→```bash
   124→cat prelude/full.l24 myapp.l24 | cor24-run --run build/repl-bare.s --terminal --stack large
   125→```
   126→
   127→**Pros**: No recompilation to switch preludes. Easy to customize.
   128→**Cons**: Slower startup (prelude sent over UART), prelude uses instruction budget.
   129→
   130→### Option C: Hybrid (recommended)
   131→
   132→Standard prelude compiled in (option A). Additional features loaded from `.l24` files:
   133→
   134→```bash
   135→# Standard prelude + lazy sequences from file
   136→cat prelude/lazy.l24 | cor24-run --run build/repl.s --terminal
   137→
   138→# Bare REPL + full prelude from file
   139→cat prelude/full.l24 | cor24-run --run build/repl-bare.s --terminal
   140→```
   141→
   142→## Migration Path
   143→
   144→1. First: Factor current `load_prelude()` into tiers (split the C strings)
   145→2. Second: Add `just run-tiny`, `just run-full` recipes
   146→3. Third: Create prelude `.l24` files for option C flexibility
   147→4. Fourth: Add `--stack` to cor24-run for full-prelude support
   148→

Let me create a comprehensive summary document. ## REPL UI Structure & Component Overview Based on my analysis of the web-tml24c codebase, here's the complete structure: ### **REPL Component Architecture** (`src/repl.rs` - 637 lines) The `Repl` struct is a Yew component that manages: **State:** - **Emulator**: `EmulatorCore` instance running the COR24 interpreter - **I/O Buffers**: - `output`: Raw UART TX output (used in Split view) - `transcript`: Interleaved UART output + echoed user input (used in CLI view) - `uart_tx_queue`: Buffered input bytes to send to emulator - **Configuration**: - `prelude`: Selected PreludeTier (Bare/Minimal/Standard/Full/Scheme) - `stack_size`: Stack allocation (3KB or 8KB) - **Runtime State**: - `running`: Whether emulator is executing - `loaded`: Whether binary successfully loaded - `waiting_for_input`: At REPL prompt ("> ") - **Memory Tracking** (live reads during emulation): - `heap_used`, `sym_used`, `str_pool_used`, `stack_depth` — sampled from memory addresses - **UI State**: - `input`: Current user input - `view_mode`: CLI or Split - `selected_demo`: Current loaded demo index - `led_on`, `switch_pressed`: Hardware state **Message Types** (`Msg` enum): - `Init` — Load binary and start - `Tick` — Animation frame tick (50K instruction batches) - `InputChanged(String)` — User typing - `Eval` — Submit input for evaluation - `SetPrelude(PreludeTier)` — Change REPL variant - `SetStack(StackSize)` — Change stack allocation - `LoadDemo(usize)` — Load a demo program - `ToggleView` — Switch between CLI and Split modes - `Reset` — Reload emulator and clear output - `ClearAll` — Clear all output and input **Key Methods:** - `load_binary()` — Assembles selected prelude tier, loads into emulator, captures memory pool addresses - `send_input()` — Queues user input bytes with newlines - `view_gauge()` — Renders colored memory usage bar (green <60%, yellow 60-85%, red >85%) - Tick loop: Feeds input bytes with poll-before-feed (matches cor24-run), runs batches, reads UART output --- ### **View Mode Switching** **CLI Mode** (single-line prompt): - Bottom bar with `"> "` prompt - Single-line text input (clears after Enter) - Interleaved transcript (shows input echoed + output) - Status message on right side **Split Mode** (multi-line editor): - Full output on left/top - Semi-transparent textarea overlay (bottom-right 50%) - Dashed golden border, blurred background - "Eval" button to submit - Used automatically when loading demos --- ### **Styling** (`src/repl.css` - 299 lines) **Color Scheme** (Catppuccin theme): - Background: `#1e1e2e`, Surface: `#282840` - Text: `#cdd6f4`, Accent: `#89b4fa` (blue), Green: `#a6e3a1` **Hardware Indicators** (floating panel, top-right): - **LED D2**: Circular indicator, radial gradient when on (`#ff0000` glow) - **Switch S2**: Square button, cyan glow when pressed - **Memory Gauges**: 4 bars (Heap, Syms, Strs, Stack) with color transitions **Toolbar**: Prelude + Stack + Demo dropdowns, buttons (Split/Resume/Reset/Clear) **Input Areas**: - CLI: minimalist bottom bar with focus underline - Split: layered textarea with semi-transparent backdrop **Spinner**: Rotating border animation (0.8s) while evaluating --- ### **Demo System** (`src/demos.rs` - 147 lines) **Demo Struct**: ```rust pub struct Demo { pub title: &'static str, pub description: &'static str, pub source: &'static str, // Loaded from tml24c/demos/*.l24 pub prelude: PreludeTier, // Which variant to use pub stack: StackSize, // 3KB or 8KB } ``` **18 Built-in Demos** (embedded via `include_str!`): **Minimal Prelude** (1 demo): - Tail-Call Optimization (countdown, sum, fibonacci) — 8KB stack required **Standard Prelude** (10 demos): - Blink LED, Bottles of Beer (2 versions), Error Handling, Macros, Metaprogramming, Multi-line Input, Mutation, Quasiquote, Strings, Variadic Functions **Full Prelude** (5 demos): - Anaphoric Macros, Bottles (Trampoline), Lazy Sequences, Threading Macros, Utility Functions **Scheme Prelude** (1 demo): - Scheme Demo (define-fn, let*, equal?, factorial) **UI Integration**: - Demos grouped by prelude in dropdown (optgroups) - Clicking demo: auto-switches to correct prelude, sets stack, resets emulator, switches to Split view - Demo source displayed in textarea, ready to edit or run --- ### **REPL Variants** (`src/config.rs` - 85 lines) 5 PreludeTiers load corresponding assemblies from `asm/` directory: | Tier | File | Description | Functions | |------|------|-------------|-----------| | **Bare** | `repl-bare.s` | Raw primitives only | ~15 built-ins (arithmetic, I/O) | | **Minimal** | `repl-minimal.s` | 6 comparison functions | Bare + `= < > <= >= !=` | | **Standard** | `repl-standard.s` | ~40 functions | Minimal + list ops, macros, strings, I/O (`map`, `filter`, `define`, `lambda`, `set!`, etc.) | | **Full** | `repl-full.s` | ~65 functions | Standard + lazy sequences, threading macros (`->`, `->>`), anaphora (`aif`, `awhen`) | | **Scheme** | `repl-scheme.s` | Scheme-flavored | `#t`/`#f`, `define-fn`, `let*`, `equal?`, `even?`/`odd?` | **Binary Files** (`asm/` directory): - Each `.s` file is ~2.2 MB of COR24 assembly - Embedded as string literals at compile time - Assembled at runtime by `Assembler::assemble()` **Stack Configurations**: - **3KB**: 0xFEEC00 — hardware default - **8KB**: 0xFF0000 — full EBR window (needed for deep recursion) --- ### **How Examples Are Loaded** 1. **Adding a new demo**: - Create `.l24` file in `tml24c/demos/` (relative path works via `include_str!`) - Add `Demo` struct entry to `DEMOS` array in `src/demos.rs` - Specify: title, description, source (via `include_str!`), prelude tier, stack size - Rebuild with `./scripts/build-all.sh` 2. **User loads demo**: - Clicks Demo dropdown → selects entry - `LoadDemo(index)` message triggers: - Sets `prelude` and `stack_size` from demo metadata - Sets `input` to demo source code - Calls `load_binary()` to assemble and load new variant - Switches to Split view for multi-line editing - User can edit code in textarea and click "Eval" to run 3. **Demo Metadata in Source**: - Comment header (e.g., `;; Prelude: standard, Stack: 3 KB`) - Not parsed by UI — manual metadata in `src/demos.rs` struct --- ### **HTML Template** (`index.html` - 99 lines) Minimal Trunk setup: - Single `
` mount point - Catppuccin color variables in `

    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    grep -v '^;;' "{{file}}" | {{cor24_run}} --run build/repl-standard.s --terminal --speed 0 -n 500000000 2>&1 | \
        grep -v -E '^Assembled |Executed [0-9]+ instructions|^\[CPU'

# Evaluate with Scheme prelude
eval-scheme file: build-scheme
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    grep -v '^;;' "{{file}}" | {{cor24_run}} --run build/repl-scheme.s --terminal --speed 0 -n 500000000 2>&1 | \
        grep -v -E '^Assembled |Executed [0-9]+ instructions|^\[CPU'

# Evaluate with full prelude
eval-full file: build-full
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    grep -v '^;;' "{{file}}" | {{cor24_run}} --run build/repl-full.s --terminal --speed 0 -n 500000000 2>&1 | \
        grep -v -E '^Assembled |Executed [0-9]+ instructions|^\[CPU'

# Evaluate with custom prelude (slow: prelude loaded via UART)
eval-custom file prelude: build-bare
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    { grep -v '^;;' "{{prelude}}"; grep -v '^;;' "{{file}}"; } | \
        {{cor24_run}} --run build/repl-bare.s --terminal --speed 0 -n 500000000 2>&1 | \
        grep -v -E '^Assembled |Executed [0-9]+ instructions|^\[CPU'

# === Tests ===

test: build
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail

## Complete Exploration Report: tml24c Project

### 1. ALL DEMO/EXAMPLE FILES

I found **21 demo files** in `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tml24c/demos/` and **3 example files** in `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tml24c/examples/`.

**Demo Files (demos/ directory):**

| File | Features Demonstrated | Prelude Required | Stack | Description |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|-------|-------------|
| `quasiquote.l24` | Quasiquote, unquote, splicing, templates | standard | 3KB | Template syntax for macro code generation |
| `tco.l24` | Tail-call optimization | minimal (bare) | 3KB | Constant-stack recursion: countdown, sum, fibonacci |
| `variadic.l24` | Variadic functions, rest parameters | standard | 3KB | Lambda with `args` and dotted pairs |
| `strings.l24` | String type, manipulation | standard | 3KB | String literals, length, append, equality |
| `macros.l24` | let, cond, and, or | standard | 3KB | Macro fundamentals and control flow |
| `anaphora.l24` | Anaphoric macros (aif, awhen, aand) | full | 3KB | Unhygienic macros that bind 'it' variable |
| `mutation.l24` | set!, closures, mutable state | standard | 3KB | Mutable assignment, counters, memoization |
| `threading.l24` | Thread-first (->), thread-last (->>)  | full | 3KB | Pipeline-style data transformation macros |
| `lazy.l24` | Lazy sequences, iterate, lazy-map/filter | full | 8KB | Infinite sequences with lazy evaluation |
| `utilities.l24` | partial, juxt, doseq, dotimes | full | 3KB | Function combinators and iteration macros |
| `multiline.l24` | Multi-line input, paren-depth tracking | standard | 3KB | REPL multi-line support demo |
| `metaprogramming.l24` | eval, macroexpand, gensym, symbol conversion | standard | 3KB | Runtime code introspection and execution |
| `continuations.l24` | catch/throw, call/ec, guard, dynamic-wind | standard+ | 3KB | Exception handling and guaranteed cleanup |
| `fixedpoint.l24` | Fixed-point/scaled integer arithmetic | standard | 3KB | Currency and measurement calculations |
| `functional.l24` | fn?, compose, complement, every?, flatten, zip | full | 3KB | Function type checking and combinators |
| `errors.l24` | Runtime error messages | standard | 3KB | Error handling demonstrations |
| `scheme.l24` | #t/#f, cond/else, let/let*, predicates | scheme | 3KB | Scheme-style naming and idioms |
| `bottles.l24` | Macros, TCO, display | standard | 3KB | 99 Bottles song (macro + recursion) |
| `bottles2.l24` | Trampoline, mutual recursion | full | 3KB | 99 Bottles via thunk trampolining |
| `bottles4.l24` | map, for-each, functional style | standard | 3KB | 99 Bottles via list processing |
| `blink.l24` | Memory-mapped I/O, delay, peek/poke | standard | 3KB | LED blink at 1Hz (requires --speed 500000) |

**Example Files (examples/ directory):**

| File | Content | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `demo.l24` | Basic arithmetic, functions, recursion, closures, macros | Simple language tour |
| `io-demo.l24` | Hex literals, peek/poke, LED control, button input | Hardware I/O demonstration |
| `prelude.l24` | (Read not fully needed, assumed empty or minimal) | Prelude loading example |

**Clojure Reference Files (not Lisp, for translation reference):**
- `bottles.clj`, `bottles2.clj`, `bottles3.clj`, `bottles4.clj` - Clojure versions for porting guide

---

### 2. RECENTLY ADDED FEATURES (from git log and CHANGES.md)

**Latest features (March 24, 2026):**

1. **Continuations & Error Handling** (most recent)
   - `catch`/`throw` - Non-local exit primitives
   - `call/ec` - Escape continuations
   - `raise`/`with-handler` - Error signaling and handling
   - `guard` - Pattern-matched error handling
   - `dynamic-wind` - Guaranteed cleanup on exit (non-local or normal)
   - `unwind-protect` - Common Lisp style cleanup
   - New demo: `continuations.l24`

2. **Functional Programming Toolkit**
   - `fn?` - Callable type predicate
   - `compose` - Function composition
   - `complement` - Negate predicates
   - `constantly` - Always-return-value function
   - `every?`, `none?` - List predicates
   - `flatten` - Recursive flattening
   - `zip` - Transpose lists
   - New demo: `functional.l24`

3. **Multi-line REPL & String Expansion**
   - Paren-depth tracking across newlines
   - String literal limit: 63 → 119 characters

4. **Metaprogramming**
   - `eval` - Runtime code evaluation
   - `macroexpand`, `macroexpand-1` - Macro introspection
   - `gensym` - Hygienic symbol generation
   - `symbol->string`, `string->symbol` - Conversions

5. **Prelude Tiering**
   - 4 prelude variants: tiny (minimal), standard (default), full (Clojure-style), scheme (R7RS-inspired)
   - Bare REPL with custom prelude support
   - `.l24` files for all preludes

6. **Earlier (still recent) Features**
   - Set! (mutable assignment)
   - Threading macros: `->`, `->>`
   - Lazy sequences: `lazy-cons`, `lazy-map`, `lazy-filter`, `iterate`
   - Utility functions: `partial`, `juxt`, `doseq`, `dotimes`
   - Anaphoric macros: `aif`, `awhen`, `aand`

---

### 3. REPL VARIANTS & FEATURES

**Four REPL Variants** (compiled from different preludes):

| Variant | File | Prelude Size | Features | Stack | Use Case |
|---------|------|--------------|----------|-------|----------|
| **bare** | `repl-bare.s` | None (custom) | Only C primitives | 3KB | Custom dialects, maximum headroom |
| **standard** (default) | `repl-standard.s` | ~3K lines | Core 60+ functions: map, filter, reduce, let, cond, and, or, etc. | 3KB | General-purpose Lisp, most demos |
| **full** | `repl-full.s` | ~8K lines | Standard + lazy seqs, threading, anaphora, function combinators, trampoline | 8KB | Complex applications, Clojure-style |
| **scheme** | `repl-scheme.s` | ~3.6K lines | Scheme idioms: #t/#f, R7RS naming (let*, equal?, even?/odd?) | 3KB | Scheme compatibility |

**Invoked via justfile:**
- `just eval demos/<file>.l24` - Uses repl-standard.s
- `just eval-full demos/<file>.l24` - Uses repl-full.s
- `just eval-scheme demos/<file>.l24` - Uses repl-scheme.s
- `just eval-custom prelude/<file>.l24 demos/<file>.l24` - Uses repl-bare.s + custom prelude

content.text:
There were transient build errors from trunk watching partial saves, but the final build succeeded. The dev server is live at **http://localhost:9135** with the CPU gauge.

Here's what was added:

**CPU Gauge** — appears in the hardware panel above the memory gauges:
- **Green** ("idle") when load < 20% — sitting at REPL prompt
- **Yellow** ("running") when 20-60% — moderate computation
- **Red** ("pegged") when > 60% — tight loops, heavy eval
- Uses EWMA (alpha=0.15) of `instructions_run / BATCH_SIZE` each tick, so it smoothly ramps up when code starts and decays back when it finishes
- Snaps to 0 instantly when the emulator stops (returns to prompt or halts)

Go test it — try the Lazy Sequences or Bottles demos to see it ramp up, and watch it drop back to idle when done.

Dev server is up at **http://localhost:9135** with all latest C code rebuilt. The demo dropdown now has 5 new entries in the Standard prelude group:

And in the Full prelude group:
- **Functional Toolkit** — compose, complement, every?, flatten, zip

All built from the latest tml24c (commit `d4856c6` with restartable conditions). Go test them.
